page: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Edsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to a single physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C core with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It includes 118 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated as average at 2215.58, while the multi-core score drops to a weak rating of 2263.98. Memory performance shows a similar trend, with an average PassMark memory score of 1927.71, but weak uncached read and write bandwidths of 22.92 GB/sec and 12.15 GB/sec respectively. For lightweight AI workloads, the server achieves an average text generation speed of 88.94 tokens/second using a small 135M LLM model, though prompt processing speeds remain weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E2ds_v7 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. The inclusion of local storage provides a resource-dense option for caching and temporary database storage. This server is best suited for small-scale databases, memory-intensive caching layers, lightweight web serving, and basic data processing tasks where memory capacity is prioritized over multi-threaded compute performance.
description: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance includes 118 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance and average PassMark memory performance, though multi-core compute and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this server qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound applications that do not demand heavy parallel processing. It is best suited for small database deployments, caching layers, and lightweight web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and 118 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads and small databases.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and local storage for memory-bound workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
118 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability

Optimized for small databases, caching layers, and memory-bound workloads
